title: Bump LLVM to e89a4dfabfac7016819d201dacb6c6c58e6a2365
body:
Most needed changes are mechanical, since upstream MLIR has switched from `OpaqueProperties` to the type-safe `PropertyRef`. We had one instance in the code (OM `ExtClassOp`) where we accidentally constructed properties for the wrong class (`ClassOp`) -- the new type-safety caught that.

This bump also surfaced a potential race condition or inconsistency in libstdc++: after the bump, I've started to see crashes related to thread pool deallocation in code that used the Slang AnalysisManager. These issues might have been there before, but have now been triggered through an unrelated change in LLVM. The crashes were all related to an exception being thrown inside `condition_variable_any::wait`. Since we don't really need to have Slang spin up its own thread pool, simply disable threading for it.

---
_Original:_

This is a clean LLVM bump.

While testing, I did notice one circt-synth test that was not gated behind a libz3 requirement, so I added that.
